name: The Modern DevOps Life Cycle
description:
The DevOps life cycle represents the continuous movement of software from planning to monitoring. It emphasizes the collaboration between Development and Operations teams through Continuous Integration (CI) and Continuous Deployment (CD). By automating the build, test, and release phases, organizations can shorten the systems development life cycle and provide continuous delivery with high software quality.

  • CI: Automating the integration of code changes into a shared repository.
  • CD: Automating the delivery of applications to selected infrastructure environments.
  • Feedback Loop: Monitoring data informs the next planning phase, closing the loop.
code:
graph LR
  subgraph SG_DEV [Planning & Coding]
    PLAN[Planning] --> CODE[Coding]
  end

  subgraph SG_CI [Continuous Integration]
    CODE --> BUILD[Build]
    BUILD --> TEST[Automated Testing]
  end

  subgraph SG_CD [Continuous Delivery]
    TEST --> RELEASE[Release Packaging]
    RELEASE --> DEPLOY[Deployment]
  end

  subgraph SG_OPS [Operations & Feedback]
    DEPLOY --> OPERATE[Operations]
    OPERATE --> MONITOR[Monitoring]
    MONITOR -.-> PLAN
  end

  style SG_DEV fill:none,stroke:#64748b,stroke-width:2px,stroke-dasharray: 5 5
  style SG_CI fill:none,stroke:#64748b,stroke-width:2px,stroke-dasharray: 5 5
  style SG_CD fill:none,stroke:#64748b,stroke-width:2px,stroke-dasharray: 5 5
  style SG_OPS fill:none,stroke:#64748b,stroke-width:2px,stroke-dasharray: 5 5

  classDef stage fill:#ffffff,stroke:#334155,stroke-width:2px,color:#0f172a,font-weight:bold
  class PLAN,CODE,BUILD,TEST,RELEASE,DEPLOY,OPERATE,MONITOR stage
